steps.title: Strashed AIG: 5 ANDs implementing f = ab + ad + cd
steps.narrative:
Abc_NtkRewrite visits every internal AND in topological order and asks: can a precomputed library subgraph replace this cone with fewer ANDs? We follow the AND at f, where the trace's gain happens. (The other four ANDs are also tried and reported as no-gain.)

Dashed edges = inverter on the wire. The PO edge from f is dashed, so PO outputs ¬f-AND = ¬(n8·¬n9) = (n6+n7)+n9 = ab+ad+cd.

panes.title: Per-node pipeline
panes.body:
1. enumerate 4-input cuts (cuts walkthrough)
  2. filter: drop cuts with >2 fanout-1 leaves
  3. for each survivor: MFFC + NPN lookup + score
  4. commit best-gain replacement

steps.title: Three cuts at f survive the fanout-1 filter
steps.narrative: Cut enumeration produces five 4-leaf cuts at f. The fanout-1 filter (rwrEva.c:122..129) drops cuts where more than 2 leaves have fanout 1 — those leaves are captive to the cone and offer no shared structure to reuse. Three survive; we'll evaluate each in turn.

panes.body: TTs come from a 4-input simulation seeded at the leaves. See the K-feasible cut and simulation vectors walkthroughs for those mechanics.

steps.title: Cut A — {a, b, c, d}
steps.narrative: All four PIs as leaves. The cone covers everything internal: MFFC = {f, n8, n7, n9, n6} = 5 ANDs. The cut's truth table is the original output function ab+ad+cd, and the cheapest known AIG for this class needs 4 ANDs (factoring a(b+d) + cd).

panes.title: Replacement (4 ANDs)
panes.body:
m1 = ¬b · ¬d   (both fanins dashed; m1 = ¬(b+d))
m2 = a · ¬m1   (= a·(b+d))
m3 = c · d
f  = ¬m2 · ¬m3   (= ¬(ab+ad+cd))

steps.title: Cut B — {a, c, d, n6}
steps.narrative:
n6 (= a·b) is treated as a leaf even though it's an internal AND — the boundary boost (abcRewrite.c:138..148) protects it during the MFFC walk, so its sub-cone (the a·b AND) is left intact. MFFC = {f, n8, n7, n9} = 4 ANDs.

TT = 0x001F = NPN class 12 directly (no phase / perm needed). The canonical for class 12 is 3 ANDs — see the NPN canonization walkthrough.

steps.title: Cut C — {a, d, n6, n9}
steps.narrative: Two internal ANDs are absorbed as leaves: n6 (= a·b) and n9 (= c·d). The MFFC shrinks to just {f, n8, n7} = 3 ANDs. TT = 0x0007, which already needs 3 ANDs to express (¬(n6 + a·d + n9)).

steps.title: Compare gains; pick the best
steps.narrative: Rwr_NodeRewrite tracks the best (cut, library-subgraph) pair across all surviving cuts using strict-better comparison. In the cut chain produced bottom-up, Cut C is visited first (gain 0, no improvement), then Cut B sets the running best at gain 1, then Cut A also yields gain 1 — not strictly better, so B holds.

panes.body: Tie-break: first-found gain wins under strict comparison. ABC's cut chain at f orders survivors as C → B → A, so Cut B is the first non-zero gain and stays best.

steps.title: Commit Cut B's replacement: 5 ANDs → 4 ANDs
steps.narrative: Dec_GraphUpdateNetwork materializes the 3 new ANDs over leaves {a, c, d, n6} and redirects the f-AND fanout (the PO) to the new top node. The 4 old MFFC nodes ({f, n8, n7, n9}) become dangling and are reclaimed by reference counting. n6 = a·b is kept — that's exactly why it was treated as a leaf.

panes.body: Result: m1 = ¬a·¬c, m2 = d·¬m1, f = ¬n6·¬m2 — equivalent to n6 + d·(a+c) = ab + ad + cd. The remaining n6 = a·b AND survives, giving 4 ANDs total.
